cards.back: A joint probability distribution is a probability distribution of two or more discrete random variables
The conditional probability of an event A given an event B is P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B) / P(B)
Two events A and B are said to be independent if P(A ∩ B) = P(A) * P(B)
The marginal probability of a random variable X is P(X) = P(X ∩ Y) / P(Y)
The joint probability mass function of two discrete random variables X and Y is P(X, Y) = P(X) * P(Y|X)
